The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. It checks the same bounds (0 <= number/need/remaining <= 1000) and returns the same result conditions: when need <= remaining, result must be [number + need, remaining - need]; otherwise result must be [number + remaining, 0]. The logical structure is equivalent for all concrete inputs.
